observations: document.title === 'Calendar - Jira' (the space name 'Autoloop' is NOT included, so the exact expected 'Calendar - {Space} - Jira' format is not met)
Space navigation contains tabs: Summary, Board, List, Calendar, Timeline, Approvals, Forms, Docs (plus '4 more tabs' overflow button)

All non-active tabs in nav[aria-label="Space navigation"] are rendered as <a> links with href (e.g. Summary -> /jira/core/projects/AUT/summary, Board -> /jira/core/projects/AUT/board, List -> /jira/core/projects/AUT/list, Timeline -> /jira/core/projects/AUT/timeline)
The Calendar tab is uniquely rendered as a role=heading level=2 with accessible name 'Calendar' (no link element) — this is the active-tab affordance
Breadcrumb shows link 'Spaces' -> /jira/projects and heading level=1 'Autoloop' identifying the project/space
Calendar main content is present: grid with weekday columnheaders Monday–Friday and gridcells for each date (e.g. 'April 23, 2026')
Toolbar above the grid shows heading level=3 'Apr 2026' and buttons 'Today', 'Previous month', 'Next month', 'Month options', 'Unscheduled work', 'View settings'
Complementary region role with accessible name 'Unscheduled work' is visible with heading level=2 'Unscheduled work'

locators.purpose: Active Calendar tab (rendered as heading when active)
Inactive Board tab (link form, proves Calendar is uniquely active)

notes: PARTIAL because the observed document.title is 'Calendar - Jira' — the space name ('Autoloop') does NOT appear in the title, so the exact expected format 'Calendar - {Space} - Jira' is not met. Active-tab signaling does NOT use aria-selected or aria-current; instead the active tab is rendered as an <h2> (role=heading level=2) inside nav[aria-label="Space navigation"] while inactive tabs remain <a> links. Spec should assert Calendar appears as a heading within the Space navigation and not as a link. The page loaded while signed in; no login redirect occurred. Some console errors were logged but did not prevent render.
